name: orchestrator
_design_notes: v8.13.1 orchestrator subagent profile. The orchestrator subagent is spawned by scripts/run-cycle.sh and constrained by three PreToolUse hooks (ship-gate, phase-gate-precondition, role-gate). allowed_tools is intentionally narrow: orchestrator can RUN scripts (via Bash(bash scripts/...:*)) but cannot Edit/Write source code (that's Builder's job inside a worktree — role-gate enforces) and cannot directly invoke git commit/push/gh release create (must go through scripts/ship.sh which ship-gate allowlists). Phase ordering is enforced by phase-gate-precondition: subagent-run.sh invocations must respect the Scout→Builder→Auditor sequence in cycle-state.json. v8.13.1 audit MEDIUM-1 fix: cycle-state.sh permissions are restricted to {advance,set-agent,get,exists,dump,path} subcommands; init+clear are denied so the orchestrator cannot self-disable the gates by removing cycle-state.json. run-cycle.sh's parent process (NOT the orchestrator subagent) handles init/clear lifecycle. v8.19.3: each kernel-script pattern has BOTH a relative variant (Bash(bash scripts/...:*) — works in dev mode where cwd=repo) AND a glob-absolute variant (Bash(bash **/scripts/...:*) — works in plugin install mode where the orchestrator uses $EVOLVE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/... after the v8.18.0 dual-root migration). The same dual-pattern approach applies to disallowed_tools so the bypass-prevention guards continue working. v8.19.5: added explicit absolute-path patterns for both marketplace (~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/<plugin>/scripts/) and cache (~/.claude/plugins/cache/<ns>/<plugin>/<version>/scripts/) install layouts. These supplement the existing relative-path and `**` glob patterns. Single `*` per segment is provably correct per Anthropic docs (`*` matches arbitrary characters including spaces — and at minimum a single path segment when adjacent to `/` literals). v8.20.0: PATH-based kernel script invocation. Dispatcher prepends $EVOLVE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts (and release/) to PATH so subagents invoke kernel scripts by bare name (cycle-state.sh advance, ship.sh, etc.). Allowlist collapsed from 140 install-layout-specific patterns to ~16 bare-name patterns. New disallowed_tools entries (unlink, ln, cp, PATH= override) close the PATH/script-substitution attack vector. Sandbox-exec write_subpaths still restricts file writes to .evolve/runs/cycle-* — orchestrator cannot create malicious scripts in any PATH directory.
_sandbox_design_notes: v8.13.7 cycle 8215-rc3: orchestrator needs OS-level write access to (a) .evolve/cycle-state.json + its _atomic_write '.tmp.$$' sibling, (b) .evolve/ledger.jsonl (subagent-run.sh appends), (c) .evolve/runs/cycle-* (its own report). cycle-state* uses a glob because sandbox-exec '(subpath FILE)' matches the file and descendants but NOT siblings sharing a prefix; generator at scripts/cli_adapters/claude.sh:265-267 dirname()s glob entries to .evolve/. To prevent that broadening from exposing other .evolve/ files, deny_subpaths explicitly enumerates EVERY top-level .evolve/ entry that is NOT one of the three intended write targets: state.json, state.json.bak, profiles/, evals/, instincts/ (parent), calibrate.sh, run_10_cycles.py, history/, research/, workspace/, genes/, tools/, skill-inventory.json, token-baseline.json, domain.json, latest-brief.json, notes.md, guards.log, current_run_id, .current_run. The .current_run dotfile must be quoted carefully — sandbox-exec subpath of a hidden filename works the same as a regular filename. Forge-resistance for ledger.jsonl is provided by hash-chain verification (cycle-health-check.sh + verify-eval.sh), NOT by the sandbox.
